title: Exotic Smoky Jamaican Chicken Skewers
description: Smoky and gooey Jamaican dish featuring clotted cream and nuka pickle, grilled to perfection on a robata grill, this meal prep friendly recipe is low in sodium and full of flavor.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together jamaican jerk seasoning, brown sugar, soy sauce, and lime juice.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, mix the clotted cream and chopped nuka pickle in a small bowl.
Once the chicken is cooked, brush the clotted cream mixture onto the skewers.
Return the skewers to the grill and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the cream is caramelized and gooey.
Remove the skewers from the grill and sprinkle with chopped scallions.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional scallions and lime wedges if desired.
